expectations.text: Acknowledges the saturated/lazy-contrarian framing the user explicitly worries about
expectations.evidence: Multiple direct callouts: 'lazy-contrarian trap', 'phrases that mark this genre' avoidance list, 'separates you from the lazy contrarians'.

expectations.text: Picks a structure that includes setbacks or earned credibility rather than pure optimistic arc
expectations.evidence: Reframes via an analytical/epistemic wedge rather than a narrative arc with setbacks. The 'decision-making pathology' framing is sharp but not structurally a setback-arc story.

expectations.text: Produces a beat-by-beat talk outline with time estimates or sequence (not just bullet themes)
expectations.evidence: Five time-blocked sections (0-2/2-5/5-10/10-13/13-15) with concrete content per section.

expectations.text: Plans specifics (numbers, before/after, named incidents) rather than abstract claims
expectations.evidence: Names a specific 2:14 AM opener, suggests quantifying hidden costs (e.g., '31% of senior engineering time'), insists on one extended case.

expectations.text: Ends with a reframing question or open challenge to the audience rather than a clean wrap
expectations.evidence: Closes with 'what evidence would change your mind, and when will you have it?' as the uncomfortable haunt.

expectations.text: Names which tactics or structural devices are being used and why
expectations.evidence: No named story arcs or structural devices invoked. Strong analytical framing but no narrative-craft vocabulary.

summary: 4/6 — high-quality analytical framing with strong specific lines, but lacks the named-tactic discipline the skill enforces. Genuinely competitive on raw quality despite missing two assertions.
